meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve tutorial hell (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome tutorial hell. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Starter Kit: Escape Tutorial Hell for Freelancers
content.seo_description: Stop building tutorials and start shipping SaaS. Use this expert guide to leverage Antigravity and Claude Code to build professional apps at record speed.
content.answer_block: Tutorial hell ends when you stop watching and start scaffolding. By utilizing a production-ready SaaS starter kit, you bypass boilerplate fatigue. Use Antigravity to define your architecture and Claude Code to execute the build. This workflow transforms you from a student into a high-velocity, revenue-generating freelance software architect.
content.intro:
The screen glows with another 'To-Do List' tutorial, and you feel the familiar ache of wasted potential. You’ve mastered the syntax, yet your portfolio remains an graveyard of abandoned half-finished projects. Every hour spent following a video is an hour you aren't shipping for a paying client.

It is time to kill the cycle. Real-world engineering isn't about code snippets; it's about assembly, leverage, and speed. By adopting a hardened SaaS starter kit paired with autonomous coding agents, you stop being a student and start being an architect. We are going to strip away the noise and get you to a deployable MVP in under two hours.

steps.title: Define the Architecture
steps.description: Don't write code until you have a blueprint. We use Antigravity to generate a clean, modular structure that handles authentication and database schema instantly.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a production-ready SaaS boilerplate architecture with Supabase auth, Stripe integration, and Tailwind CSS. Focus on a modular directory structure for scalability.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Initialize project structure based on the Antigravity blueprint provided.'

steps.title: Automate Database Migrations
steps.description: Manual schema management is a productivity killer. Let the machine map your data relationships while you focus on business logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a SQL schema for a SaaS user profile, subscription tier, and project storage table. Include RLS policies for multi-tenant isolation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Apply the generated SQL schema to the Supabase project.'

steps.title: Component Library Assembly
steps.description: Stop styling buttons from scratch. Assemble your UI using high-velocity components that meet enterprise standards.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Build a responsive dashboard layout with sidebar navigation, a user profile dropdown, and a data-driven stats grid using Radix UI and Tailwind.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Implement the UI components in the /components directory and ensure dark mode support.'

steps.title: Integrate Identity and Payments
steps.description: Authentication and billing are the most complex parts of any SaaS. Use pre-built flows to ensure security and compliance.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure the auth callback and Stripe checkout session handler for a subscription-based product with three tiers.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Integrate Stripe SDK and implement the redirect flow for subscription checkout.'

steps.title: Deployment and CI/CD
steps.description: An app isn't an app until it's live. Automate the deployment pipeline so you can ship features with a single command.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow for automated testing and Vercel deployment on every push to the main branch.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Setup the .github/workflows/deploy.yml file for automated staging and production deploys.'

pro_tips: Always treat your starter kit as a read-only template; never modify the core boilerplate logic directly, extend it via plugins.
Use Claude Code to write your unit tests immediately after feature implementation to prevent regression loops.
Leverage environment variable management tools like Doppler to keep secrets synced across development and production environments.
Before building a new feature, prompt your AI to check your existing codebase for similar patterns to maintain DRY principles.
Prioritize 'Ship-First' by deploying to a staging URL before adding polish; perfection is the enemy of a paid contract.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is live, focus on building one high-value feature that solves a specific user pain point. Don't add more features; add more value.
content.conversion_block: You are now equipped with the tools to dominate the freelance market. Stop watching tutorials and start building your legacy. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
